title: Is Death and Rebirth worth owning
display_title: Is Death and Rebirth worth owning
wikitext:
[]: Is "Death and Rebirth" worth owning?             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Mon Jul 25, 2011 11:30 am My perception is that it doesn't have anything that EoE doesn't have, and less of it. Even so, it's still Evangelion. I also found it for cheap someplace. Should I score it?

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon Jul 25, 2011 11:33 am Not really. Only if you're a collector.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 25, 2011 11:51 am I own it. It has some interesting editing choices and differences in music and animation. It appeals to be in that sense, and I found it for cheap as well. If that's what appeals to you, or if you're like Eva Monkey and have to own every American DVD release of Eva, then I'd say go for it. But if you're never gonna watch it, then why bother?

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Mon Jul 25, 2011 12:01 pm I own it simply because I bought the boxset. The R1 DVD really doesn't have anything worth owning on it. The picture and audio quality are pretty lackluster and the special features consist of pointless dub bullshit. Unless you really want to sit down and watch the AWL commentary, which isn't even as entertainingly bad as it's chalked up to be. (it's more just inoffensively dull and full of typical dub wankery like, "Yeah this actor we clearly grabbed off the street, that even fans of the Eva dub dislike, did such an amazing job in this role, they're such a great actor," and, "I changed this sound effect because I felt like it,".)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jul 25, 2011 12:55 pm I think Death is a good recap of the series. It puts you in the right frame of mind to watch The End of Evangelion, without having to watch the entire series. That said, don't spend too much on it, it's not worth very much.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 25, 2011 1:39 pm I think I found my copy from somewhere between $20 - $40. I probably wouldn't spend more on it than that if I had to.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Jul 25, 2011 1:39 pm Pirate a subbed (or raw)version of Death. It leads into EoTV quite well, but the dub's picture is low video quality, it has changed sound effects, and the commentary isn't as good as EoE's. By the way, I rarely tell people to pirate things. I only do it or advise people to do it if it's out of print, has a bad export, or no export at all. It may be illegal, but I see nothing morally reprehensible in pirating it.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Mon Jul 25, 2011 1:40 pm I'd have to say no. If you really want to watch it, try to see if you can find it online.

[]:             Quote Post by McDirty » Mon Jul 25, 2011 4:21 pm Pretty much what everyone's said so far. Unless you're a collector, don't bother. However, I do recommend Eva fans watch Death at least once for the experience. Death was actually my very first exposure to Evangelion and it certainly influenced me to go and watch the entire series. The string quartet scenes are among my personal favorite anime sequences and Evangelion doesn't feel complete to me without them.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Jul 25, 2011 4:25 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I think Death is a good recap of the series. It puts you in the right frame of mind to watch The End of Evangelion, without having to watch the entire series. That said, don't spend too much on it, it's not worth very much.

[]:             Quote Post by MandarinOrange » Mon Jul 25, 2011 7:35 pm I watched it and was fond of it. It was a nice lead up to EoE without going thru a marathon of watching the series. And I really liked how SI looked :B

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Tue Jul 26, 2011 12:03 am I like it. I agree with the above that it's kind of a nice break to review the series before jumping to EoE. I like the soundtrack too.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jul 26, 2011 1:19 am Rebirth proper isn't on Manga Ent's release, anyway, just "ReAir". Death is excellent to own though.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Jul 26, 2011 2:19 am At current used prices (under $10 @ Amazon ) I'd say it's worth it. As SSD said, what is claimed to be "Rebirth" on the US DVD is actually the corresponding footage from "Air" (Ep. 25', aka the first half of EoE) with the title cards and ED credits from Rebirth pasted in the appropriate places. This contrasts with the Japanese DVD release of Death and Rebirth, which had the actual footage from Rebirth . Still, Death is a good recap as such things go; the classical music used is nice, as is the ED song (Tamashii no Refrain). Just don't spend more than $15 for it.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Tue Jul 26, 2011 11:30 pm Not really. Death is basically a recap of the series while Rebirth is the First Half of EOE.

[]:             Quote Post by Kenshin Sephiroth » Wed Jul 27, 2011 1:27 am I'd definitely recommend Death , although it being my first exposure to the franchise probably constitutes the basis of my bias. Nevertheless, I uphold it to be a truly well-edited and well-executed atmospheric summary of the series that provides an effective "Eva fix" to those inclined. Nothing ground-shaking or breath-taking, but a good watch worth buying if the price is right. Don't bother with Rebirth , it's just the first half of EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon Jul 25, 2011 11:33 am Not really. Only if
